purpose: For the purpose of measurement and payment, the actual dimensions of the RCC members shall be taken. The purpose of payment, however, the clarification as given in para a, b & c above shall apply. For the purpose of determining exposure conditions, all places within a distance of 10 kms. of coastal line, sea front would be treated as coastal area. The purpose of this specification is to give smooth and even surface true to line and form.
scope: The scope is defined as applicable to "chapter 03 mortars and chapter 04 concrete work". This includes considerations for both "mortars" and "concrete work" as outlined in the respective chapters. The scope encompasses the requirements and guidelines stated in these chapters, specifically "chapter 03" and "chapter 04", as applicable.
acronyms:
Design mix concrete is defined as "that concrete in which the design of mix i.e. the determination of proportions of cement, aggregate & water is arrived as to have target mean strength for specified grade of concrete." 

Additionally, "any additional compliance criteria" shall be declared, which includes "definition, tolerance frequency of assessment, method of test and significance result." 

Damp Proof Course is mentioned, but its definition is Not explicitly stated in the specification.

procedure:
While the drum is rotating, water shall be added gradually to achieve the water cement ratio as specified or as required by the Engineer-in-Charge. The following stepwise procedure shall be followed:

1. After adding water, the mixing shall be continued until concrete of uniform colour, uniformly distributed material and consistency is obtained.
2. Mixing shall be done for atleast two minutes after adding water.
3. If there is segregation after unloading from the mixer, the concrete should be remixed.
4. The drum shall be emptied before recharging.
5. When the mixer is closed down for the day or at any time exceeding 20 minutes, the drum shall be flushed cleaned with water.

For hand mixing, the procedure is as follows:
1. Read on top of cement sand mixture and mixing done by showlling and turning till the coarse aggregate gets evenly distributed the cement sand mixture.
2. Three quarters of the total quantity of water required shall be added in a hollow made in the middle of the mixed pile and the material is turned towards the middle of pile with spade.
3. The whole mixture is turned slowly over and again and the remaining quantity of water is added gradually.
4. The mixing shall be continued until concrete of uniform colour and consistency is obtained.
5. The mixing platform shall be washed and cleaned at the end of the day.

For obtaining the coarse aggregate in different sizes and blending them in right proportions, the following steps shall be followed:
1. The different sizes being stocked in separate stock piles.
2. The material should be stock-piled for several hours preferably a day before use.
3. The grading of coarse and fine aggregate should be checked as frequently as possible, the frequency for a given job being determined by the Engineer-in-Charge to ensure that the specified grading is maintained.

A competent person to interpret the specified requirements and relate these to mix design criteria shall systematically review specification and orders supplied by the purchaser. These shall be formally recorded together with any modification to the specification resulting from subsequent agreed documentation to ensure that the plant operator is given the correct instructions for batching and mixing.

The concrete mix proportion chosen should be such that the concrete is of adequate workability. The degree of workability and slump values for different placing conditions are as follows:
- Blinding concrete: shallow sections: Pavements using pavers: Very low 
- Mass concrete: Lightly reinforced sections in slabs, beams, wall, columns, : floors: Low: 25-75
- Hand placed pavements: canal lining; Strip footing: Medium: 50-100 

Note: The values for other placing conditions are Not explicitly stated in the specification.
equipment:
The following equipment are mentioned: 
- Back up pump of rigid and flexible pipes of varying lengths 
- Curved sections of rigid pipes 
- Swivel joints and rotary distributors 
- Pin and gate valves 
- Switch valves 
- Connection devices 
- Splints, rollers, and other devices 
- Transitions for connecting different sizes of pipe sections 
- Air vents 
- Clean out equipment 
- Concrete pump 
- Mixer 
- Measuring equipment 
- Analogue scale displays 
- Digital readouts 
- Weighing device 
- Discharge screw 
- Batching console 
- Weigh-dial or display.
personnel:
The following roles are explicitly mentioned: 
"Engineer-in-Charge" and "Manufacturer/ Contractor". 
The "Engineer-in-Charge" is responsible for approving test results, directing intervals for testing water, and providing directions for various construction activities. 
The "Manufacturer/ Contractor" is responsible for identifying and informing the "Engineer-in-Charge" about the location of the source of water intended to be used.
quality:
Test on cube crushing strength of concrete shall be done in accordance and compliance with IS 456 and IS 516. The average of the strength of three specimen shall be accepted as the compressive strength of the concrete provided the strength of any individual cube shall neither be less than 70% nor higher than 130% of the specified strength. 

Each sample shall be of adequate quantity so that a minimum of 3 specimen cubes can be made test of the sample in accordance with IS 516. All test specimens shall be made compacted cured and tested in compliance with IS 516 and test result interpreted in accordance with IS 456 for acceptance of concrete strength.

28 days’ test shall have to be carried out to determine the compressive strength of concrete cubes if the actual average strength of sample accepted is lower than the specified or higher by more than 15%. In addition to 28 day crushing strength test, early strength tests at 7 days shall also be conducted on field specimens as well as guideline test specimens.
health_safety: Safety measures to prevent impact of traffic, scour due to water etc. should be taken. Adequate precautionary measures shall be taken to prevent accidental impacts etc. Safety device shall be provided to secure the hopper in raised position when not in use. Clutch brake and hydraulic control lever shall be designed so as to prevent displacement by liberation or by accidental contact with any person.
other: ded for the use in structures located in earthquake zone subjected to severe damage and for structures subjected to dynamic loading (other than wind loading) such as railway and highway bridges. The producer shall have a procedure in place to enable the diagnosis and correction of faults identified from complaints. area of each openings/ obstructions does not exceed 0.4 square metre. Work in or under water, Work in liquid mud, Work in or under foul positions. RCC work shall be done carefully so that the thickness of plaster required for finishing the surface is not more than 6 mm.
